bd.title: Government Unveils BDT 7.97 Trillion Budget Targeting Inflation and Job Creation
bd.summary: Finance Minister Abul Hassan Mahmood Ali has presented a Tk 7.97 trillion national budget for the fiscal year 2024-25, focusing on controlling inflation, generating employment, and revitalizing the economy. The ambitious budget aims to navigate global economic uncertainties while fostering sustainable growth and development across key sectors. This budget is crucial for addressing ongoing economic challenges faced by the country.

bd.title: Inflation Remains Elevated at 9.74% in May, Fueling Cost of Living Concerns
bd.summary: Bangladesh's national inflation rate slightly decreased to 9.74% in May, a marginal drop from the previous month, yet remaining significantly high for consumers. Food inflation continues to be the primary driver, putting immense pressure on household budgets and posing a persistent challenge for the government's economic management strategies. The situation impacts daily lives and consumer purchasing power across the nation.

bd.title: Persistent Power Cuts Plague Bangladesh Amidst Rising Demand and Fuel Shortages
bd.summary: Bangladesh is grappling with widespread power outages across various regions as extreme temperatures lead to surging electricity demand, compounded by ongoing fuel supply constraints. Officials attribute the load shedding to insufficient gas imports and maintenance issues at several power generation plants. The persistent power crisis is causing public frustration and impacting both daily life and industrial production.

international.title: Ukraine Intensifies Drone Attacks on Russian Territory as Western Allies Consider Expanding Aid
international.summary: Ukraine has reportedly increased its drone attacks targeting military and energy infrastructure deep inside Russian territory, marking an escalation in its defensive strategies. Concurrently, Western allies are actively discussing new packages of military and financial aid, including potential permission for Ukraine to use Western-supplied weapons on Russian soil. This comes amidst ongoing fierce fighting and strategic positioning on the eastern and southern fronts.

international.title: Israel's Rafah Offensive Intensifies Amid International Condemnation and Humanitarian Crisis Warnings
international.summary: Israeli forces have escalated their military operations in Rafah, southern Gaza, despite widespread international condemnation and a recent International Court of Justice ruling. The intensified offensive is leading to a deepening humanitarian crisis, with hundreds of thousands of displaced Palestinians facing severe shortages of aid and essential services. Global pressure is mounting for an immediate ceasefire and protection of civilians.

international.title: India's Ruling BJP Alliance Secures Third Term, Narendra Modi Set for Swearing-in as Prime Minister
international.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) has won India's general election, securing enough seats to form a government for a third consecutive term. Narendra Modi is preparing to be sworn in as Prime Minister, although the BJP's reduced majority signals a stronger opposition presence and potential shifts in political dynamics. The outcome impacts regional geopolitics and India's economic trajectory.
